message.content:
You are an independent, adversarial fix-confirmation reviewer (reviewer A) for the hc-assay hardening campaign at /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay (branch harden/pass-10, latest commit). Confirm the fix is BOTH appropriate (correct root cause, sound approach, right layer) AND complete (no residual gap; regression test DISCRIMINATES — fails if reverted). Be skeptical; find holes.

FINDING P10-CI-1:
require_green_ci.sh checked only the latest CI run's conclusion, not its headSha → a stale green run from an earlier commit certified a newer untested HEAD (fix-regression of CI-1's gate). Fix: fetch headSha; compute head_sha=git rev-parse origin/<branch> (fallback <branch>); if run_sha != head_sha exit 1 (CI for this commit not complete); else require completed/success. VERIFY by reading the script and reasoning: does it fail closed when the latest run is for a different/older SHA? does it still pass on a genuinely-green current HEAD? Test: test_merge_gate_compensating_control_exists asserts headSha + rev-parse present. Edge: shallow clone / origin ref missing (fallback to local <branch>).

Read the changed files and run what you can (cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && source .venv/bin/activate && python -m pytest -q <path>; "python -m pytest"; gh for CI). Judge ONLY P10-CI-1. CONFIRMED only if appropriate AND complete; CONCERN if a real residual gap; REJECTED if wrong/non-discriminating. List concrete concerns (empty if none).

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
d890ed3 fix(hardening-p10): 7 confirmed incl. 4 HIGH (2 security, 1 privacy, 1 provenance)
17b4eb5 docs(hardening): pass-9 merged (#170); open pass 10
a49b3f6 Merge pull request #170 from [REDACTED]/harden/pass-9
edbb576 docs(hardening): record pass 9 — actual-CI-execution + fix-regression each caught a HIGH
22cbcad fix(hardening-p9): wire ASSAY_SCRUB_HOSTS into the capture hook so [REDACTED] actually scrubs (P9-PII-1 concern)
---BRANCH---
harden/pass-10

1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# Pre-merge CI gate (#CI-1) — compensating control for an unenforceable required status check.
3	#
4	# Branch protection / required status checks are UNAVAILABLE on this repo's plan: it is private on
5	# a free tier, so `gh api repos/<owner>/<repo>/branches/main/protection` returns HTTP 403
6	# ("Upgrade to GitHub Pro or make this repository public"). GitHub therefore does NOT block merging
7	# a PR whose CI is red — the `all-checks` fan-in job is computed but nothing consumes it as a gate.
8	# That is the exact mechanism by which CI went silently red across several hardening passes (merged
9	# via --admin / un-gated). Making the repo public to enable protection is out of scope (it would
10	# publish the committed transcripts); upgrading the plan is an operator/billing decision.
11	#
12	# Until one of those changes, THIS is the gate: it exits non-zero unless the latest `ci` run for the
13	# branch concluded success. Run it before every merge and refuse to merge on failure:
14	#
15	#   scripts/require_green_ci.sh [branch]      # defaults to the current branch
16	#   scripts/require_green_ci.sh && gh pr merge <n> --merge --delete-branch
17	set -euo pipefail
18	
19	branch="${1:-$(git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD)}"
20	# The SHA the green run MUST match (#P10-CI-1): checking only the latest run's conclusion let a
21	# STALE green run (from an earlier commit) certify a newer, untested HEAD — the gate must bind the
22	# green result to the exact commit being merged, and fail closed if no completed run exists for it.
23	head_sha="$(git rev-parse "origin/$branch" 2>/dev/null || git rev-parse "$branch")"
24	read -r status conclusion run_sha < <(
25	  gh run list --branch "$branch" --workflow ci.yml --limit 1 \
26	    --json status,conclusion,headSha \
27	    -q '.[0].status + " " + (.[0].conclusion // "none") + " " + (.[0].headSha // "none")'
28	)
29	echo "latest ci run for '$branch': status=$status conclusion=$conclusion sha=$run_sha (HEAD=$head_sha)"
30	if [ "$run_sha" != "$head_sha" ]; then
31	  echo "::error::latest CI run is for $run_sha but '$branch' HEAD is $head_sha — CI for this commit has not completed; do NOT merge (#P10-CI-1)." >&2
32	  exit 1
33	fi
34	if [ "$status" = "completed" ] && [ "$conclusion" = "success" ]; then
35	  echo "CI is green for the current HEAD — safe to merge."
36	  exit 0
37	fi
38	echo "::error::CI for '$branch'@$head_sha is not green (status=$status conclusion=$conclusion) — do NOT merge (#CI-1)." >&2
39	exit 1
40

d890ed3 fix(hardening-p10): 7 confirmed incl. 4 HIGH (2 security, 1 privacy, 1 provenance)
8c5d7e5 fix(hardening-p9): 5 confirmed incl. security regression of pass-8 unix-socket fix
---DIFF---
commit d890ed3cd1afc6f960ba9bfa434c16842d122fae
Author: [REDACTED] <[REDACTED]>
Date:   Wed Jun 17 13:34:33 2026 -0400

    fix(hardening-p10): 7 confirmed incl. 4 HIGH (2 security, 1 privacy, 1 provenance)
    
    All 2-agent verified TRUE_POSITIVE. Two are fix-regressions of pass-9's own fixes.
    
    - SEC-10-1 (high): libpq PGHOST/PGHOSTADDR/PGSERVICE env vars override/supply the host even
      when the DSN looks loopback (PGHOSTADDR wins over host=localhost) → graph state off-box
      despite require_local_uri passing. Fix: _assert_local_libpq_env() validates PGHOST/PGHOSTADDR
      (loopback/socket, comma-split) and rejects PGSERVICE/PGSERVICEFILE (a service file can
      redirect off-box, unvalidatable inline), called from get_postgres_connection_string.
    - P10-PII-1 (high): the operator's real LAN topology (gateway [REDACTED], host
      [REDACTED], interface) leaked into committed transcripts from captured route/lsof
      output — no IP rule. Fix: RFC1918 redaction (10/8, 172.16/12, 192.168/16) with digit/dot
      lookarounds (catches the \n-glued form, never matches inside a longer number); loopback
      127.x, 0.0.0.0 and public IPs preserved. Re-scrubbed corpus (literal private IPs now 0).
    - P10-CI-1 (high, fix-reg of CI-1): require_green_ci.sh checked only the latest run's
      conclusion, not its headSha → a stale green run certified a newer untested HEAD. Fix:
      fetch headSha and require it == git rev-parse origin/<branch>; fail closed if no completed
      run for the current commit.
    - P10-MO-1 (high): a set/frozenset payload is hashable but does NOT round-trip
      (unfreeze→list→re-freeze→tuple) so verify_records SILENTLY failed on a rebuilt chain. Fix:
      ProvenanceEntry rejects set/frozenset payload leaves loudly at record time (use a sorted
      list/tuple, which round-trips and digests identically).
    - P10-MO-2 (med): from_records raised a raw AttributeError on a non-mapping payload field.
      Fix: ProvenanceEntry raises typed ProvenanceError when payload is not a Mapping.
    - P10-PII-2 (low, fix-reg of P9-PII-1): the machine-hostname rule lacked a leading \b →
      substring over-redaction (host 'host' redacted inside 'localhost'). Fix: leading \b.
    - REL-1 (low, fix-reg of pass-8 K-REL-1): SIGTERM handler swallowed the signal when
      getsignal() returns None (prior handler installed from C). Fix: treat None like SIG_DFL and
      re-deliver the default terminate.
    
    Regression tests for every finding. Gates: ruff + format + mypy --strict clean; 575 passed,
    5 skipped; coverage floor enforced.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <[REDACTED]>

+  exit 1
+fi
 if [ "$status" = "completed" ] && [ "$conclusion" = "success" ]; then
-  echo "CI is green — safe to merge."
+  echo "CI is green for the current HEAD — safe to merge."
   exit 0
 fi
-echo "::error::CI for '$branch' is not green (status=$status conclusion=$conclusion) — do NOT merge (#CI-1)." >&2
+echo "::error::CI for '$branch'@$head_sha is not green (status=$status conclusion=$conclusion) — do NOT merge (#CI-1)." >&2
 exit 1

content.text: Now let me run the test and verify discrimination by analyzing the logic. Let me run the test and verify the script syntax.

Test passes. Now let me verify the discrimination claim: does the test fail if reverted? The reverted script would lack `headSha` and `rev-parse`.

The original (reverted) script: `read -r status conclusion` — no `headSha` in json, no `rev-parse "origin/$branch"`. The test asserts `"headSha" in body` and `"rev-parse" in body`. Let me confirm the reverted version had neither.

input.command: cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && git show 8c5d7e5:scripts/require_green_ci.sh 2>/dev/null | grep -c "headSha\|rev-parse" || echo "checking..."; echo "---"; git show 8c5d7e5:scripts/require_green_ci.sh 2>/dev/null | grep -n "headSha\|rev-parse" || echo "NEITHER PRESENT in reverted version"

content.text:
Important finding: the reverted (pre-fix) script at line 19 already contains `rev-parse` (`git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD`). So the `"rev-parse" in body` assertion does NOT discriminate — it was already true before the fix. Let me verify the headSha assertion does discriminate.

The pre-fix version has zero `headSha`. So `"headSha" in body` DOES discriminate — the test would fail if reverted because headSha is absent. The `rev-parse` assertion is non-discriminating but the headSha assertion alone is sufficient to make the test fail on revert.

Let me confirm: if I revert just the diff, does headSha disappear entirely?

input.command: cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && git show 8c5d7e5:scripts/require_green_ci.sh | grep -c "headSha"; echo "=count of headSha in pre-fix version (expect 0)"

content.text:
Confirmed: the test discriminates via the `headSha` assertion (0 occurrences pre-fix → assertion fails on revert). The `rev-parse` assertion is redundant/non-discriminating but harmless.

Now let me reason through the runtime logic for fail-closed behavior and edge cases.

**Logic analysis:**

1. `head_sha=$(git rev-parse "origin/$branch" 2>/dev/null || git rev-parse "$branch")` — resolves the SHA being merged. Edge: shallow clone / missing origin ref → falls back to local `$branch`. Reasonable.

2. `run_sha` comes from `gh run list ... headSha`, with `// "none"` fallback if no run exists.

3. **Stale-run case**: latest run is for an older commit → `run_sha != head_sha` → exit 1. Fail-closed. Correct.

4. **No-run case**: `gh run list` returns empty → run_sha="none". `head_sha` is a real SHA → `"none" != <sha>` → exit 1. Fail-closed. Correct.

5. **In-progress for current HEAD**: run_sha == head_sha but status != "completed" → falls through to final echo → exit 1. Correct.

6. **Genuine green current HEAD**: run_sha == head_sha, status=completed, conclusion=success → exit 0. Correct.

Let me check one potential gap: the fallback `git rev-parse "$branch"` for the bare branch name when origin ref is missing.

input.command: cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && echo "local HEAD sha:"; git rev-parse "harden/pass-10"; echo "origin sha:"; git rev-parse "origin/harden/pass-10" 2>/dev/null || echo "(origin ref absent - fallback to local)"
input.description: Check SHA resolution and fallback path

content.content:
local HEAD sha:
d890ed3cd1afc6f960ba9bfa434c16842d122fae
origin sha:
origin/harden/pass-10
(origin ref absent - fallback to local)

content.text:
The fallback works. Let me consider the one subtle gap worth flagging: there is a potential mismatch between what the gate compares (`origin/<branch>` or local `<branch>` HEAD) and what `gh pr merge` would actually merge. If origin ref exists but is stale relative to local (unpushed commits), the gate compares against origin SHA while CI may have run on a newer pushed commit — but that's actually the correct binding since CI runs on pushed commits. The fallback to local `$branch` when origin is absent could over-pass if local is ahead of what was pushed/tested, but in that scenario `gh run list` would report the pushed SHA which wouldn't match local HEAD → exit 1. So still fail-closed.

One more: word-splitting safety. `run_sha`/`head_sha`/`status`/`conclusion` are all single tokens from `read`, quoted in comparisons. The `read` with process substitution — if the `gh`/`git` subshell fails under `set -e`/`pipefail`, behavior is worth noting, but a `gh` failure produces empty output → `run_sha="none"` via the jq `//` fallback only applies inside jq; if `gh` itself errors, `read` gets empty fields. With empty `run_sha`, `"" != <head_sha>` → exit 1. Still fail-closed.

The fix is appropriate (correct root cause — binding green to commit SHA, right layer — the gate script) and complete (fail-closed on stale/missing/in-progress runs; passes on genuine green HEAD). The test discriminates via the `headSha` assertion.

input.rationale:
P10-CI-1 fix is appropriate and complete. Root cause is correctly addressed at the right layer (the gate script scripts/require_green_ci.sh): the gate now binds the green CI result to the exact commit being merged. It resolves head_sha via `git rev-parse origin/<branch>` (fallback to local `<branch>` for shallow clone / missing origin ref), fetches the latest run's headSha via `gh run list --json ... headSha`, and exits 1 when run_sha != head_sha BEFORE checking conclusion.

Fail-closed reasoning verified across cases: (1) stale green run for an older commit -> run_sha != head_sha -> exit 1; (2) no run for the commit -> jq `// "none"` yields run_sha=none != head_sha -> exit 1; (3) gh error -> empty run_sha != head_sha -> exit 1; (4) run for current HEAD but status != completed -> falls through to final exit 1; (5) genuine green current HEAD (run_sha==head_sha, completed, success) -> exit 0. All correct. `bash -n` passes; variables are single tokens and quoted in comparisons.

Regression test discriminates: the pre-fix script (commit 8c5d7e5) contains zero `headSha` occurrences, so `assert "headSha" in body` FAILS if reverted. Test passes on the fixed tree (1 passed).
concerns: Non-discriminating sub-assertion (not blocking): `assert "rev-parse" in body` was ALREADY true in the pre-fix script (line 19: `git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD`), so that assertion alone does not detect a revert. The accompanying `headSha` assertion is what makes the test discriminate, so discrimination is preserved, but the rev-parse assertion gives false confidence and could pass a regression that drops the SHA-resolution while keeping the abbrev-ref call. Tightening it (e.g. assert `rev-parse "origin/` or `rev-parse "$branch"`) would harden the test.

Minor edge (does not break fail-closed): when origin/<branch> is absent the gate falls back to LOCAL branch HEAD; if local is ahead of what was pushed/tested, the local SHA won't match the pushed run's headSha and the gate exits 1 (correct), but the operator gets a SHA-mismatch error rather than a clearer 'push your commits first' message. Behavior is safe, only the diagnostic could be friendlier.

content.text:
Reviewed P10-CI-1. Verdict: CONFIRMED. The fix binds the green CI result to the exact commit SHA (headSha from `gh run list` vs `git rev-parse origin/<branch>` with local fallback), fails closed on stale/missing/in-progress runs, and passes only on a genuinely green current HEAD. The regression test discriminates via the `headSha` assertion (absent pre-fix). One non-blocking note: the `rev-parse` sub-assertion was already satisfied pre-fix and is non-discriminating on its own.

Relevant files:
- /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/scripts/require_green_ci.sh
- /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/tests/test_supply_chain.py (test_merge_gate_compensating_control_exists, lines 339-354)
